symbols: cross-topped staff — preaching of the Gospel
dog with torch — Domini Canis (hound of the Lord)
white habit with black cloak — Dominican religious order
globe — worldwide mission of the order
description: A full-length portrait of Saint Dominic de Guzmán standing in an arched niche framed by red curtains. He wears the white habit and black cloak of the Dominican order, holds a cross-topped staff in his right hand and a lily branch in his left. A halo glows around his head. At his feet, a black-and-white dog holds a burning torch in its mouth beside a globe. The composition is formal and devotional.
theological_description: Saint Dominic de Guzmán (1170-1221) founded the Order of Preachers (Dominicans), dedicated to combating heresy through education, preaching, and the pursuit of truth. The dog with a torch (Domini Canis — 'hound of the Lord') represents the order's mission to set the world alight with the truth of the Gospel. The lily signifies Dominic's personal purity and devotion. The cross-staff symbolizes his commitment to preaching Christ crucified. Dominic's emphasis on study, prayer, and preaching shaped Catholic intellectual life profoundly, producing great theologians like Thomas Aquinas.

scripture_refs.reason: Go and make disciples — the preaching mission Dominic embraced
Preach the word, be prepared in season and out of season
